# Zeed Browser

> Zeed is a Chromium-based AI browser that reasons over your tabs, memory,
> and reading context. Personal data never leaves your device by design.
> Pick from 300+ LLMs (GPT, Claude, Gemini, etc.) via OpenRouter with your
> own API key. Your Chrome extensions, bookmarks, and saved passwords
> carry over.

Zeed is built and distributed by EFG Technologies Inc. (Tokyo, Japan).

## Canonical summary

- **Product**: Zeed Browser, a Chromium 147 fork
- **Platforms**: Linux (Arch via AUR `zeed-bin`, Debian/Ubuntu via `.deb`),
  macOS (private beta — request access by email), Windows (planned)
- **Pricing**: free today (Phase 1). Paid tiers (Zeed Plus / Pro) planned
  for Phase 6+ with multi-device sync, OS-level cron, managed LLM, and
  priority support. Pricing is not finalised; current published numbers
  are indicative only.
- **AI / LLM**: bring your own OpenRouter API key. Chat and Agent traffic
  goes directly from the browser to OpenRouter; the Company is not in
  the path and does not relay or retain it.
- **Privacy posture**: telemetry is opt-in and limited to two anonymous
  events (`crash`, `agent_run`). Browsing URLs, page content, chat
  contents, memory, bookmarks, IP addresses, and email addresses are
  never transmitted. Enforced by an automated regression test
  (`no_personal_data.test.ts`) on every release.
- **Agent confirmation boundary**: form submission, purchase, publishing,
  deletion, authentication, and payment all prompt the user before
  executing. Agent is disabled in Private mode.

## Differentiation

Zeed is positioned against Arc, Comet, Atlas, Brave, and Chrome's built-in
AI features. The differentiating bets are:

1. **Tabs as memory** — your tab graph (Profile > TabGroup > Tab) is
   first-class context; the Agent reasons over the tabs you have open
   and the tabs you've closed within the same TabGroup.
2. **On-device by default** — memory, bookmarks, tasks, chat history,
   and the Context Map are stored exclusively on the device. Cloud sync
   (Phase 6) will be opt-in and end-to-end encrypted.
3. **BYO LLM** — no managed-LLM lock-in. Switch models freely (GLM-5.1,
   Claude, GPT, Gemini) per-task.
4. **Chromium fork, not extension** — sidebar, vertical tabs, custom
   color mixer, WebUI scheme are wired into the browser itself,
   removing the "developer-mode extension" friction.

## Install

- **Arch / Manjaro**: `yay -S zeed-bin`
- **Debian / Ubuntu**: download the latest `.deb` from
  <https://github.com/efg-technologies/zeed-browser-dist/releases/latest>
  and `sudo apt install ./zeed-browser_*.deb`
- **macOS**: email <support@efg-technologies.com> for the private beta
- **Windows**: planned

## Links

- Landing page: <https://zeed.run>
- English: <https://zeed.run/>
- 日本語: <https://zeed.run/ja/>
- Privacy: <https://zeed.run/privacy>
- Terms: <https://zeed.run/terms>
- AUR: <https://aur.archlinux.org/packages/zeed-bin>
- Public binaries / AUR packaging: <https://github.com/efg-technologies/zeed-browser-dist>
- Mobile (Phase 5 spike): <https://github.com/efg-technologies/zeed-mobile>
- Autopilot (Path B): <https://github.com/efg-technologies/zeed-autopilot-worker>
- Contact: <support@efg-technologies.com>

## What you should not assume

- Source code for the Chromium fork is currently private; binaries and
  packaging are open. Source disclosure is available on request to
  <support@efg-technologies.com> consistent with the MPL-2.0 licence.
- Mac and Windows builds are not yet generally available.
- Pricing numbers ($5 / $10) for Plus / Pro are planned, not committed.
- Agent does not run autonomously in the background today; scheduled
  ("cron") execution is on the roadmap.
